notes: Antigravity workflow fan-out, UNBLOCKED by subagent linking. The parent uses its multi-agent feature (invoke_subagent with a Subagents array holding several entries) to fan out THREE parallel children in one call. Each child runs as its OWN top-level brain/<child>/ conversation (transcript_new per child), all linked to the SAME parent via AntigravityParentConvID -> parent_linked per child. LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-20: with each child instructed to `sleep 15` first, all three children are caught WORKING simultaneously while the parent is held working. UNLIKE claudecode there is NO per-run subdir and NO journal/bookkeeping file — children are flat top-level conversations — so the 'journal/unknown never surfaces as a phantom session' guard is VACUOUSLY satisfied for antigravity. The fan-out children have no own agy process (pid=0, transcript-first) — assert ParentSessionID linkage + the N-count, not child PIDs. CAVEAT (recording window): agy's fan-out children take well over the practical recording window to fully finish and be swept; this recording captures the fan-out, the per-child parent_linked, and all three children reaching working under a held-working parent, but ends before the children-finish + parent-settle-to-ready + sweep-to-zero tail. The sweep-on-done path is the same child-liveness sweep proven by cell 3.4 (orphan cleanup) and 3.1 (single child swept on parent-done). Model harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> ('Gemini 3.1 Pro'). Tokens/cost NOT in the antigravity JSONL.

text: Parent conv-id session appears in ready when transcript.jsonl is written

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: The workflow opt-in prompt drives the parent ready -> working as the fan-out starts, and the parent stays working for at least the children's parallel lifetime (held on hasActiveChildren — no flip to ready/waiting while the fan-out runs)

The first fan-out child's own conversation transcript materializes at brain/<child>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState for the child (two more children materialize in the same window)

AntigravityParentConvID binds each child.session_id -> the SAME parent.session_id (children are subagents of the parent, NOT top-level sessions). SubagentCount(parent) peaks at 3 while children run; no 'journal'/'unknown' phantom session arises (vacuous for antigravity — no per-run subdir or bookkeeping file).

At least one fan-out child is caught WORKING (each child's `sleep 15` keeps it alive long enough for the observer to see working) — proves the children actually run in parallel under the held parent, not instant no-ops
